chain.thought:
Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about the year when the Czech Republic competed in the Olympics and had the best finish. We need to know whether each competition was an Olympic event and its position. We extract the value from column "competition" and create a different column "is_olympics" for each row. The datatype is String.

Select relevant rows.
The question aims to find out in which year the Czech Republic competed in the Olympics and achieved their best position. We need to look at the rows that mention "olympic games" in the competition column and compare their positions to determine the best result.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
the year -> year
compete in the olympics -> competition
finish best -> position
column value of the question link to columns:
olympics -> competition
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
czech republic compete -> competition
finish best -> position

The question is asking to determine in which year the Czech Republic competed in the Olympics and achieved their best finishing position. To find out, we need to sort the data based on the "position" column in the order of "small to large" using the f_sort_column(position) operation. This will allow us to see the best (numerically smallest) position achieved by the Czech Republic in the Olympics for each year's data.

The Czech Republic competed in the Olympics in 2008 and finished 10th, and in 2012 they finished 8th. The best finish between these two years was 8th in 2012. Therefore, the year when the Czech Republic competed in the Olympics and finished best is 2012.

judge_thought:
Explanation: The question asks for the year the Czech Republic competed in the Olympics and achieved their best finish. Looking at the original table, the relevant Olympic years for the Czech Republic are:
2008: Finished 10th (5.45 m)
2012: Finished 8th (5.65 m)
The best finish is in 2012 with an 8th position.
Therefore, the Prediction Answer of 2012 is correct.
